data.title: Intel Stock (NASDAQ:INTC) Slides Despite New G-Series Potential
data.description:
Yesteday, we noted that chip stock Intel (INTC) was bringing its Arc G-Series processor to new gaming handhelds from Acer, the Predator Atlas 8. This revelation prompted some to wonder if they G-series lineup could be a big new source of revenue for Intel in a time when all eyes are on its bottom line. Investors seemed oddly concerned, and pulled back, sending Intel shares down nearly 2.5% in Friday afternoon's trading.

Memorial Day Sale – Claim 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock trusted, data-backed investing tools with TipRanks Premium, from analyst ratings and forecasts to breaking news and portfolio analysis.
  • Discover high-conviction stock picks and new investing opportunities with the TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter

The new chips bring together potent graphics capabilities alongside highly efficient power management systems. That combination makes them tailor-made for handheld gaming systems, where graphics demand balances on a razor's edge against battery demand. Intel's G-Series has a string of other tools that help out in this regard, including an available Xbox mode to offer a console-style experience, and Intel Precompiled Shaders to make games load faster.

Naturally, Intel does not have this market to itself. Both Qualcomm (QCOM) and AMD (AMD) have chips in this space. Qualcomm's Snapdragon Elite Gaming system is proving to be a significant presence, and AMD has perennially been an Intel competitor. The Ryzen Z2 processor is proving to be a powerhouse in portable gaming, so Intel will not have things all its own way here.

Keeping Things Cool

Perhaps sensing the upcoming summer heat in North America, and knowing that people will spend a large portion of it indoors, Intel recently offered up a set of new Linux patches geared toward Directed Package Thermal Interrupts. Intel first sent out patches addressing these interrupts back in March, and a second version landed just recently.

Directed Package Thermal Interrupts allow package thermal interrupts to be sent to one core alone. This in turn allows for fewer cores getting involved, less demand for resources, and a reduced need to accidentally fire up idle cores when only one core could do the job. So far, reports suggest, only newer Intel processors will have access to Directed Package Thermal Interrupts, and several upcoming processors—Nova Lake and Diamond Rapids—will have it on board as well.

Is Intel a Buy, Hold or Sell?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on INTC stock based on 10 Buys, 25 Holds and three Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 518.36% rally in its share price over the past year, the average INTC price target of $86.94 per share implies 26.41% downside risk.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: SpaceX Secures $4.16 Billion Space Force Contract Ahead of IPO
data.description:
The U.S. Space Force has awarded Elon Musk's SpaceX a $4.16 billion contract for space-based satellites ahead of the company's upcoming initial public offering (IPO).

Memorial Day Sale – Claim 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock trusted, data-backed investing tools with TipRanks Premium, from analyst ratings and forecasts to breaking news and portfolio analysis.
  • Discover high-conviction stock picks and new investing opportunities with the TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter

Specifically, the contract is for the Space-Based Airborne Moving Target Indicator program, which is designed to track airborne threats to the U.S. from space. Going forward, SpaceX will provide global tracking capabilities to U.S. military forces.

Government officials in Washington, D.C. say the program addresses challenges faced by the U.S. military as adversaries develop increasingly sophisticated missiles and airborne attack systems, many of which can bypass traditional detection systems.

"By focusing these capabilities to the space domain, we are providing the Joint Force with sustained battlespace awareness of contested airspace," said Col. Ryan Frazier, acting Space Force portfolio acquisition executive for SBST.

The SB-AMTI system integrates space-based sensors, communication links, and ground processing. The Space Force is using a hybrid acquisition model combining an OTA agreement with an Indefinite Delivery/Indefinite Quantity structure.

The Space Force has established a multi-vendor framework for the program. SpaceX and other companies were selected for the vendor pool through competitive OTAs announced by Secretary of the Air Force Troy Meink at Space Symposium in April.

The initial award is projected to deploy a satellite constellation by 2028. The Space Force plans to issue additional awards in the coming year to expand the program's capacity.

Space Systems Command manages a $15.6 billion annual space acquisition budget for the Department of War.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: 777X in 2027? FAA Suggests So, as Boeing Stock (NYSE:BA) Rises
data.description:
More good news hit for aerospace stock Boeing (BA) as the Federal Aviation Administration seems ready to start clearing new Boeing aircraft. The outlook was optimistic, and investors followed along, sending Boeing shares up modestly in Friday afternoon's trading.

Memorial Day Sale – Claim 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock trusted, data-backed investing tools with TipRanks Premium, from analyst ratings and forecasts to breaking news and portfolio analysis.
  • Discover high-conviction stock picks and new investing opportunities with the TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter

You might remember yesterday, when Boeing CEO Kelly Ortberg noted that the 737-7 was likely to be certified ahead of the 737-10. That sentiment was echoed, at least somewhat, by FAA administrator Bryan Bedford. Bedford noted that Ortberg's projections were right, saying that the MAX 7 was likely to come first, followed by the MAX 10. But Bedford took things one step farther, saying, "…hopefully the 777 early next year."

This came as a bit of a surprise, as Boeing was looking for an amended type certificate before the end of 2026. That would have opened up the potential for deliveries to start in early 2027. But if the certification does not arrive until 2027, then that likely means the delays will continue. The flight test department's workload has been "heavy," reports note, and with two other planes likely to be certified ahead of the 777X, that may explain why.

Repurposed Hardpoints

Meanwhile, reports note that Boeing is working to get more life out of the B-1B lineup by improving weapons capacity. Using a new feature call the Load Adaptable Modular multi-use weapons pylon, Boeing could effectively turn bombers in missile-carriers as well, especially long-range hypersonic missiles, reports note.

The pylon has not yet been certified, but Boeing is working on getting through the design phase that will see formerly-used hardpoints—points where weapons can be mounted—replaced with the new pylon. The pylon has been tested as far back as 2022, and tests continued into 2024, finding that the B-1B could indeed carry long-range munitions.

Is Boeing a Good Stock to Buy Right Now?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on BA stock based on 15 Buys and one Hold assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 10.35% rally in its share price over the past year, the average BA price target of $274.14 per share implies 18.64% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: Why Blue Origin's Explosion Is Hammering Space Stocks
data.description:
Space stocks dropped sharply on Friday after Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket exploded during a test on a Florida launchpad. The accident was a harsh reminder that the space industry can be extremely risky, as investors have been chasing the sector ahead of SpaceX's expected IPO. New Glenn was being prepared for future satellite launches, including missions connected to Amazon's (AMZN) Leo network, which is expected to compete with SpaceX's Starlink. The failure could now delay Blue Origin's launch plans and raise questions about its role in NASA's lunar base program, which it was selected for earlier this week.

Memorial Day Sale – Claim 70% Off TipRanks

200% short exposure to AMZN with AMZO

In addition, the damage spread across several popular space stocks. AST SpaceMobile (ASTS), which has contracts with Blue Origin to launch communications satellites, fell as much as 21%, while Rocket Lab (RKLB), Redwire (RDW), Karman Holdings (KRMN), Voyager Technologies, and Intuitive Machines (LUNR) also dropped. As a result, Bloomberg Intelligence analyst John Butler said that New Glenn will likely be grounded while the company investigates what went wrong, which could put AST SpaceMobile behind schedule for its 2026 launch plans.

That is the key issue for investors. Many space stocks have been trading on excitement, momentum, and future earnings potential rather than current profits. So when a setback threatens to delay launches, revenue, or profitability, investors often sell first and ask questions later. It also doesn't help that five-star Deutsche Bank (DB) analyst Bryan Kraft downgraded AST SpaceMobile to Hold from Buy because he believes that Blue Origin's delay could make it harder for AST to meet its 2026 targets.

Is ASTS Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on ASTS stock based on three Buys, six Holds, and two Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average ASTS price target of $86.13 per share implies 22.4% downside risk.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: S&P500 Seasonality Signals an Uncertain Outlook for June
data.description:
The S&P 500 (SPX) is set to finish May with a roughly 5% return as optimism over a resolution to the U.S.-Iran war and falling oil prices drove stocks higher. However, June could bring turbulence for the benchmark index.

Memorial Day Sale – Claim 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock trusted, data-backed investing tools with TipRanks Premium, from analyst ratings and forecasts to breaking news and portfolio analysis.
  • Discover high-conviction stock picks and new investing opportunities with the TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter

In midterm years dating back to 1950, June has historically been the worst month for the S&P 500, according to Carson Investment Research. At the same time, the index has posted a positive return in June during nine of the past 10 years with an average return of 1.9%. 2022 was the sole negative year with a loss of 8.4%.

June Seasonality Shows Mild Gains and Bullish Bias

June has been a relatively quiet month over the past 20 years, finishing higher 65% of the time with an average return of 0.2%, according to Equity Clock.

Another statistic that would favor the bulls is that the market is currently in its fourth year of the bull market. The average S&P 500 return during the fourth year is 14.6%, while the median is 13.7%. The index has returned 10.4% year-to-date, suggesting that more upside could be on the way.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: NetApp Stock (NTAP) Soars 27% despite Analysts' Hold Ratings after Blowout Q4
data.description:
NetApp's (NTAP) shares skyrocketed by 27% on Friday afternoon as analysts flooded in with price target raises after the company delivered stronger-than-expected fiscal 2026 fourth-quarter earnings on May 28. However, analysts overall remain moderately cautious, with most maintaining their Hold ratings on NTAP.

Memorial Day Sale – Claim 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock trusted, data-backed investing tools with TipRanks Premium, from analyst ratings and forecasts to breaking news and portfolio analysis.
  • Discover high-conviction stock picks and new investing opportunities with the TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter

For context, NetApp provides intelligent data management software and storage infrastructure solutions to enterprises. It offers cloud storage and data services on-premises as well as private and public cloud infrastructure.

NetApp Delivers Record Performance in Q4

During the final quarter that ended on April 24, the California-based company posted record net revenue, gross profit, operating income, and cash flow from operations. Its adjusted revenue climbed by 13% to $1.95 billion, surpassing the Wall Street expectations of $1.87 billion.

NetApp's adjusted earnings per share hit $2.43, up by 26% from the same period in fiscal 2025 and above analysts' estimates of $2.27. In addition, the company's net income expanded by 22% year-over-year to $483 million.

Looking ahead, NetApp expects its net revenue to reach between $7.3 billion and $7.6 billion. The company also announced a new $1 billion share buyback program.

What Are Analysts Saying?

Reacting to the results, the following analysts maintained their Neutral ratings on NTAP stock but lifted their price targets as follows:

  • UBS analyst David Vogt raised his price target by 42%, from $113 to $160, implying about 11% downside risk ahead. Vogt said the revenue came in about 2% higher than its forecast and pointed to the company's expectations that demand will remain healthy into its new fiscal year.
  • Wedbush analyst Matt Bryson elevated his price target by 30%, from $115 to $150, implying about 16% downside risk. Bryson believes the company's outlook for the new fiscal quarter was tempered despite market conditions that should allow it to boost its revenue by increasing the price of its storage systems.
  • Bank of America's Wamsi Mohan raised his price target by 20%, from $125 to $150, suggesting a 16% downside risk. Mohan believes the company can offset the short-term impact of component cost by shifting focus to its flash and public cloud business segments.
  • Susquehanna's Mehdi Hosseini lifted his price target by 68%, from $110 to $185, suggesting about 3% upside. He noted that NetApp's guidance suggests its product gross margins may be hitting their lowest point and ready to record improvement. He believes that this view might be somewhat premature, as his team expects the markets for NAND flash memory and solid-state drives to experience more supply constraints into the second half of 2026.

Is NetApp a Good Stock to Buy?

Across Wall Street, NetApp's shares carry a Moderate Buy consensus rating from analysts. This is based on five Buys, nine Holds, and one Sell assigned over the past three months.

Moreover, the average NTAP price target of $158 implies about 12% downside risk ahead.
data.author: Solomon Oladipupo

data.title: This Is Why Hub Cyber Security Stock (HUBC) Was Up Today
data.description:
Hub Cyber Security (HUBC) stock underwent a massive rally on Friday alongside a new filing with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC). This filing revealed a major stake held by an investor in the Israel-based cybersecurity company. According to this filing, Andre Wang holds a 15.6% stake in Hub Cyber Security via his 200,000 shares.

Memorial Day Sale – Claim 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock trusted, data-backed investing tools with TipRanks Premium, from analyst ratings and forecasts to breaking news and portfolio analysis.
  • Discover high-conviction stock picks and new investing opportunities with the TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter

Wang isn't the only investor who has revealed a large stake in Hub Cyber Security stock recently. Tyler White and Jon Walden also revealed their stakes in the company on Wednesday. White holds 450,000 shares, which represent a 35.1% stake in the company. Walden owns 128,022 shares, which grants him a 9.9% stake in Hub Cyber Security.

While these recent shareholder filings may tempt some investors to buy HUBC stock, there are some red flags to note. That includes a noncompliance letter from the Nasdaq (NDAQ). Hub Cyber Security is in violation of Nasdaq Listing Rule 5250. This is because the company has yet to file its Form 20-F annual report for the period ended December 31, 2025 with the SEC.

HUBC Stock Movement Today

Hub Cyber Security stock was up 300% on Friday. However, the shares were still down 99.85% year-to-date and have fallen 99.99% over the past 12 months.

HUBC stock trading activity today was heightened. This saw some 1.16 billion shares change hands, compared to a three-month average daily trading volume of about 657,000 shares.

Is Hub Cyber Security Stock a Buy, Sell, or Hold?

Turning to Wall Street, traditional analyst coverage of Hub Cyber Security is lacking. Fortunately, TipRanks' AI analyst Spark has it covered. Spark rates HUBC stock as Neutral with a $2.50 price target. It cites "severe financial weakness (declining revenue, ongoing losses, negative equity, and negative free cash flow)" as reasons for this stance.
data.author: William White

data.title: Microsoft Stock (NASDAQ:MSFT) Surges Despite Urgent FBI Warning for All Teams Users
data.description:
Tech giant Microsoft (MSFT) has no shortage of offerings. And a new, urgent warning from the Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) in the United States actually underscores just how deep Microsoft's product line is. For anyone using Teams, Outlook or OneDrive, the FBI warning is meant for you. This did not dampen investors' spirits, though, and Microsoft shares surged nearly 4% in Friday afternoon's trading.

Memorial Day Sale – Claim 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock trusted, data-backed investing tools with TipRanks Premium, from analyst ratings and forecasts to breaking news and portfolio analysis.
  • Discover high-conviction stock picks and new investing opportunities with the TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter

A new cyberattack kit known as Kali365 has been released, the warning notes, and with it, even hackers with comparatively low skills can get access to users' accounts. With Kali365, the users can capture "Microsoft authentication tokens," which in turn can be used to bypass multifactor authentication. With the tokens, users do not even need access to a password to carry out the attack.

The kit itself offers a subscription price for hackers, and anyone willing to pay as little as $250 a month for the necessary access can put the tools therein to work. These tools include AI-generated phishing lures, dashboards to track specific entities, and OAuth token capture systems. And with hundreds of Kali365 attacks already seen, the threat is already starting to show up.

Hard Choices

Yesterday, we noted that brand awareness and support are mounting around Microsoft, and this was generally good news. Microsoft will particularly need some goodwill as it is reportedly poised to make "hard choices" about the future of Microsoft Gaming.

That line comes at a difficult time for Microsoft. It is about to walk into summer, which is basically showtime for Microsoft. Summer Game Fest, the various E3-inspired press events…this is a big time for Microsoft to show off what is coming. But we also know that video gaming in general is on something of a decline as attention gets diluted. And with chip shortages driving up prices, gaming is also much less accessible as a hobby these days. The "hard choices" could mean anything. And while current-generation gaming is safe, and the Xbox Helix is likely fine for now, gaming itself at Microsoft could be doomed down the line.

Is Microsoft a Buy, Hold or Sell?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on MSFT stock based on 34 Buys and two Holds assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 7.25% loss in its share price over the past year, the average MSFT price target of $556.88 per share implies 25.62% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson
